title: caltha auriculata
intro: Caltha auriculata, also known as the golden marsh-marigold, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Caltha auriculata include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema.
